error.signature: AI ignores Chinese guanxi (關係) in business relationship advice

verdict.summary: Chinese business operates on guanxi (relationships), mianzi (face), and renqing (reciprocal obligations). Transactional Western approaches without relationship-building fail systematically in China.

dead_ends.action: Propose business deals without established personal relationship in China
dead_ends.why_fails: In China, guanxi precedes business. Cold outreach or contract-first approaches fail because trust must be built through personal relationships, shared meals, and mutual connections before any deal.
dead_ends.condition: Entering Chinese market or seeking Chinese partnerships

dead_ends.action: Cause someone to lose face (丢面子) publicly in Chinese business
dead_ends.why_fails: Mianzi (面子/face) is the currency of Chinese social life. Public criticism, correcting someone in front of others, or rejecting a proposal bluntly destroys the relationship permanently.

dead_ends.action: Decline favors or gifts without offering reciprocation
dead_ends.why_fails: Renqing (人情) is the system of reciprocal obligations. Accepting a favor creates a debt that must be repaid. Refusing to reciprocate, or refusing a favor without good reason, breaks the guanxi network.

workarounds.action: Invest in relationship-building: shared banquets (宴请), baijiu drinking, gift exchange, visiting during Chinese New Year. Business follows relationship, not the reverse. Budget time and money for guanxi.
Give face (给面子): praise achievements publicly, use proper titles, defer to seniority, accept suggestions graciously. Save criticism for private one-on-one conversations with careful framing.
Maintain your guanxi network: remember birthdays, send festival greetings (especially Chinese New Year), reciprocate favors promptly, introduce useful contacts to your Chinese partners.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Sensitive topics are political restrictions while guanxi is the fundamental social operating system of Chinese business
